items.title: Musk v. Altman Kicks Off, DOJ Guts Voting Rights Unit, and Is the AI Job Apocalypse Overhyped?
items.summary: In this episode of Uncanny Valley, we get into how the Elon Musk-Sam Altman trial goes way beyond their rivalry and could have major implications both for OpenAI and also the AI industry at large.

items.title: MAGA Is Confused About ‘Animal Farm’
items.summary: The new film is the latest adaptation of George Orwell’s novel. Based on the critiques coming from right-wing influencers, it seems few of them fully grasp the book’s message.

items.title: Rivian downsizes DOE loan to $4.5B, while boosting capacity of Georgia factory
items.summary: Rivian has reworked its loan deal with the Department of Energy and now expects to borrow $4.5 billion to build its new factory in Georgia, down from the original amount of $6.6 billion.

items.summary: The Nobel Peace Prize laureate has been in detention since she was ousted in a military coup in 2021.

Right now, we are trying to broker a peace,” Johnson told NBC News in the…

items.title: Scientists reveal the best exercise for knee arthritis pain relief
items.summary: A major review of 217 trials shows that aerobic exercise is the most effective option for managing knee osteoarthritis. Activities like walking, cycling, and swimming outperformed other exercise types in reducing pain and improving movement. While alternatives like strength training and mind-body exercises help, they are best used alongside aerobic workouts. The findings also confirm that exercise is a safe and essential part of treatment.

items.title: Earth is splitting open beneath the Pacific Northwest, scientists say
items.summary: For the first time, scientists have watched a subduction zone literally fall apart beneath the ocean floor. Using advanced seismic imaging, they found the Juan de Fuca plate splitting into fragments as it sinks beneath North America. Rather than collapsing all at once, the plate is tearing piece by piece, like a train slowly derailing. The finding helps explain ancient plate fragments and could refine how scientists understand earthquake behavior.

items.title: Scientists restore memory by blocking a single Alzheimer’s protein
items.summary: Researchers have identified a new potential weapon against Alzheimer’s: blocking a protein called PTP1B. In mice, this approach boosted memory and helped brain immune cells clear harmful plaque buildup. Since PTP1B is also linked to diabetes and obesity—both risk factors for Alzheimer’s—it could offer a broader treatment strategy.

items.title: Cows’ methane burps may be fueled by a newfound organelle in gut microbes
items.summary: In cows’ guts, ciliates contain a tiny organelle called a hydrogenobody that may drive production of methane, a potent greenhouse gas.

items.title: Can AI help doctors avoid missed diagnoses? A new study suggests yes
items.summary: AI may help doctors avoid missed diagnoses, but it still needs real-world testing and human oversight before it can guide patient care.

items.title: Prenatal surgery for spina bifida may get a boost from stem cells
items.summary: A clinical trial for spina bifida treatment suggests that a surgical approach relying on stem cells is safe for patients. Its efficacy is still being evaluated.
